caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses right shoulder rotator cuff tendinopathy.
The clinician suspects a possible rotator cuff tear.

evidence.span_text: After reviewing the patient's examination and radiographic findings today, I have had a lengthy discussion with the patient in regards to his current symptoms. I have explained to him that his x-rays did not reveal any signs of a fracture. I have recommended that we obtain an MRI of the right shoulder to evaluate for a possible rotator cuff tear. I have also recommended that the patient attend formal physical therapy for 6 to 8 weeks. He can continue taking Tylenol as needed for pain. If his symptoms do not improve with physical therapy, I will recommend a cortisone injection.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses bilateral foot pain.
The clinician assesses right shoulder pain.

evidence.span_text: In regards to his bilateral feet pain, I have recommended that we treat the patient conservatively. I am recommending the patient soak his feet in an Epsom salt soak as well as elevate as needed.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician recommends a cortisone injection if symptoms do not improve.
